description_long: This is a single-story wooden cabin nestled among tall pine trees, featuring a sprawling, L-shaped footprint with a prominent gabled roof. The roof is dark and steeply pitched, with multiple intersecting sections and deep overhangs, providing shelter to the entryways and porch areas. The exterior walls are clad in natural wood, giving the structure a rustic and inviting appearance, while large windows are distributed asymmetrically across the facade, allowing ample natural light inside. A wide, covered porch extends along the front and side, supported by wooden posts and finished with a brick or stone base. The cabin is set on a grassy plot, with the surrounding trees providing shade and privacy. The overall design blends harmoniously with its natural environment, evoking a sense of warmth and seclusion.
description: A rustic, L-shaped wooden cabin with a dark, multi-gabled roof, large windows, and a wide covered porch, surrounded by tall pine trees on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, wide covered porch, and the prominent gabled roof with deep overhangs, framed by tall pine trees.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the length of the cabin, the continuation of the porch, and the dense arrangement of trees along the side.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals additional windows, the L-shaped layout, and the extension of the roof over the back section, with trees closely bordering the structure.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the cabin's integration with the landscape, showing the porch wrapping around the corner and the open grassy area in the foreground.
attribute_roof_type: multi-gabled roof with steep pitches, deep overhangs, and intersecting sections, clad in dark asphalt shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple large rectangular windows of varying sizes, asymmetrically distributed across the front and side facades, some grouped in pairs and others standalone
attribute_door_placement: one main exterior door centered on the front facade beneath the porch, with possible secondary doors on the side but not clearly visible
